Obtain complete color photography of Harley 4196 and Cotton Tiberius E VII.
Record every H-only, T-only, conjectural, and normalized reading without treating the printed heading as a direct-source assertion.
openQuestions.question: At the individual-reading level, how did Lovewell derive her complete Tiberius-labeled text from Horstmann's H/T edition, and where does she depart from Harley, Tiberius, Kölbing, or Horstmann?

next_steps: Search Lovewell, Albert S. Cook, Yale Studies in English, Bodleian, British Museum, and Trinity correspondence or archives.
Compare the printed streams against complete Bodley images and requested Cleopatra images.
Keep the unnamed working documents as explicit intermediaries until their identities or non-survival are established.

next_steps: Collate Lovewell's Latin text and punctuation against both complete Grässe scans.
Inspect bibliography wording, distinctive errors, and page-break correspondence.
Retain both cited editions as distinct sources until an exact copied issue is demonstrated.
openQuestions.question: Did Lovewell copy the Latin comparison from Grässe's 1846 first edition, 1850 second edition, another issue, or a mixed working source?

next_steps: Request each whole physical manuscript, including binding, covers, edges, flyleaves, blanks, and all leaves, while separately identifying the target loci.

Record any quote, delivery format, license, and exact object coverage before adding redistributed files.

reviewNotes: The abstract South English Legendary, expanded Northern Homily Cycle, Scottish Legendary, and Legenda Aurea works are modeled separately from their manuscript witnesses, printed editions, digital encodings, and repository artifact.

Lovewell's printed heading 'Bodley 799' is corrected to current shelfmark Bodley 779. Her Scottish heading's '364a-358b' is contradicted by its own internal milestones and the Cambridge catalog; the current locus is ff. 354r-360v.

The Tiberius-labeled stream contains internal milestones for ff. 192 and 193, the Harley 4196 locus. Tiberius itself is fire-damaged and incomplete at ff. 271rb-273va; Horstmann prints a complete H/T reconstructed text with H and T apparatus.

The current British Library holding record describes the Northern Homily Cycle broadly at ff. 101r-281r; DIMEV v1.0 supplies the exact Cecilia locus ff. 271rb-273va, while Horstmann's historical heading gives ff. 271-274b. These layers are recorded without treating the holding catalog as exact-locus evidence.
Yale's IIIF object has 404 canvases, but its downloadable PDF has 405 pages because page 1 is a generated metadata sheet. Cecilia canvases 337-344 therefore map to PDF pages 338-345.

Lovewell's statement that Stowe 946 was omitted conflicts with the current DIMEV census, which identifies the relevant South English Legendary witness as Stowe 949. The omitted witness is not treated as an edition source.

lineage.summary: CME00145 is the repository copy of a University of Michigan electronic transcription of printed pp. 72-102 of Bertha Ellen Lovewell's 1898 comparative edition of Saint Cecilia traditions. The encoded selection is not one medieval work: its six streams represent four works, with three witness versions of one South English Legendary life, one Latin Legenda Aurea life, one expanded Northern Homily Cycle life, and one Scottish Legendary life. Lovewell's Ashmole stream depends on Furnivall's printing; her new Bodley 779 and Cotton Cleopatra D IX streams came through unidentified library-made working transcripts; Laud Misc. 108 and Trinity R.3.25 supply apparatus readings; the Scottish stream depends on Horstmann and Metcalfe; and Lovewell's pp. 92-102 directly transcribe Horstmann's complete Harley/Tiberius edited Northern text rather than the fire-damaged Tiberius witness alone. Complete public digital objects are cached for Ashmole 43, Bodley 779, Laud Misc. 108, Trinity R.3.25, and Yale Takamiya MS 54, each with the Cecilia locus mapped inside the whole manuscript. Official catalogs, request routes, and locally cached catalog evidence are also recorded for collateral South English Legendary witnesses.

catalogingSubject.scope_note: The edition-level selection represented by University of Michigan CME item CME00145: title-page paratext and printed pages 72-102 of Bertha Ellen Lovewell's 1898 monograph. Its six encoded streams represent four medieval works: three witness versions of one South English Legendary Cecilia life, one Latin Legenda Aurea life, one editorial Harley/Tiberius representation of the expanded Northern Homily Cycle life, and one Older Scots Scottish Legendary life. This record does not collapse those works, their physical witnesses, Lovewell's complete monograph, the U-M encoding, or the repository XML into a single medieval work.

extent.description: Four medieval works are represented in six body streams: one South English Legendary Cecilia life appears in 260-line Ashmole, 257-line Bodley, and 241-line Cleopatra versions; the other works are a Latin prose life, a 464-line Northern life with a short Latin quotation paragraph, and a 634-line Scots life. The XML as a whole has 1,856 L, 340 NOTE, 30 MILESTONE, 63 PB, 10 P (seven in FRONT title matter and three in BODY), and seven HEAD elements. The verse-line total is a digital stream metric and intentionally includes all three South English witness representations; it is not a sum of four abstract-work lengths or normalized critical lineation.

formStatements.description: Three verse works and one Latin prose work represented through five vernacular verse streams and one Latin prose stream, with prose editorial apparatus.
Three rhymed vernacular saints' lives represented through five encoded streams totaling 1,856 XML line elements.
Continuous Latin hagiographic prose encoded in two body paragraphs.

notes: This manifest catalogs the six-stream edition selection. The four abstract medieval works and five vernacular representations remain explicit rather than being flattened into one title-author-date record.
The XML's 1,856 line elements, zero line-group elements, and apparatus counts are representation metrics, not normalized critical totals or stanza claims.

Lovewell's italic letters within brackets mark editorially supplied or emended letters. Determining the exact manuscript appearance behind each intervention requires comparison with the facsimiles and source editions recorded in the lineage manifest.
The complete cached manuscript images are source-research assets in the ignored source-cache directory. Their presence does not grant repository redistribution or print-on-demand rights.

Manuscript production dates, repositories, image rights, reproduction request routes, and source-cache fixity remain authoritative in the linked lineage manifest rather than being duplicated wholesale here.

summaries.value: Four medieval works about Saint Cecilia's noble Roman birth, vow of chastity, conversion of Valerian and Tiburtius, confrontation with Almachius, martyrdom, and burial are represented through six comparative streams. Three streams are witness versions of one South English Legendary life; the others reproduce the Latin prose Legenda Aurea life, an expanded Northern Homily Cycle life through a Harley/Tiberius editorial tradition, and the Older Scots Scottish Legendary life.

In order: Ashmole 43 South English Legendary text with Laud and Trinity variants; full Latin Legenda Aurea comparison; Bodley 779 South English Legendary version; Cotton Cleopatra D IX South English Legendary version; a Tiberius-labeled Northern version whose complete reading depends on Harley 4196 and Horstmann's H/T edition; and the Cambridge Gg.2.6 Scottish Legendary version.

CME00145 is not the complete 1898 monograph. It encodes the title page and comparative text pages 72-102, omitting Lovewell's preface, historical and grammatical introductions, separate variants and notes, glossaries, bibliography, and index outside that range.

The printed headings are not uniformly reliable manuscript-source labels. 'Bodley 799' is Bodley 779; the Gg.2.6 folio range is corrupt and the current target is ff. 354r-360v; and the complete 464-line 'Tiberius' stream carries Harley folio milestones and directly follows Horstmann's combined H/T edition. The South English, Latin, Northern, and Scots works therefore remain four separately counted works. The three South English witness streams are zero-count representation children of their one abstract work, and the title page is zero-count paratext.

assertion.notes: Supplied to distinguish the selected six-stream edition object, which represents four medieval works, from any one Cecilia life.
